---
title: "About Me"
image: /images/profile.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mdastgheib/
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/mohdasti
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: CV
      href: /CV/CV_MDastgheib.pdf
    - icon: ai-google-scholar
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=SNVpHcUAAAAJ
format:
  html:
    output-file: index.html
    css:
      - ../assets/css/main.css
      - ../assets/css/research-skills.css
---

I am a cognitive neuroscientist and human factors researcher focused on a core question: why does performance degrade under pressure, and where in the decision process does it happen? My work sits at the intersection of experimental psychology and applied human factors, using multi-modal methods to study how physical effort, arousal, and aging shape perception and memory.

The central tool is a handgrip dual-task paradigm—concurrent isometric grip with auditory and visual discrimination tasks—combined with pupillometry and hierarchical Bayesian drift-diffusion modeling. The goal is not a single headline metric, but an interpretable decomposition of dual-task costs that can inform the design of adaptive systems, interfaces, and human factors evaluations.

```{=html}
<div class="about-snapshot">
  <div class="snapshot-item">
    <i class="fas fa-graduation-cap"></i>
    <span>PhD 2026 (Expected), UC Riverside</span>
  </div>
  <div class="snapshot-item">
    <i class="fas fa-map-marker-alt"></i>
    <span>Los Angeles / Riverside, CA — Open to Relocation</span>
  </div>
  <div class="snapshot-item">
    <i class="fas fa-briefcase"></i>
    <span>Human Factors · Quant UXR · Research Scientist (XR)</span>
  </div>
  <div class="snapshot-item">
    <i class="fas fa-language"></i>
    <span>English (Professional) · Persian (Native) · French (Basic)</span>
  </div>
</div>
```

---

## Education

[Neurotree profile](https://neurotree.org/neurotree/tree.php?pid=860425){style="font-size:0.88rem;"} <span style="font-size:0.82rem;opacity:0.75;">(academic genealogy)</span>

::: {.edu-entry}
::: {.edu-logo}
<a href="https://www.ucr.edu" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
  <img src="/images/ucr.png" alt="University of California, Riverside logo" />
</a>
:::
::: {.edu-body}
### PhD in Psychology and Cognitive Neuroscience
[University of California, Riverside]{.edu-org}

[2021 — 2026 (Expected)]{.edu-years}
:::
:::

::: {.edu-entry}
::: {.edu-logo}
<a href="https://www.queensu.ca" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
  <img src="/images/queensu.png" alt="Queen's University logo" />
</a>
:::
::: {.edu-body}
### M.Sc. in Psychology
[Queen's University]{.edu-org}

[2017 — 2020]{.edu-years}
:::
:::

::: {.edu-entry}
::: {.edu-logo}
<a href="https://www.yorku.ca" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
  <img src="/images/yorku.png" alt="York University logo" />
</a>
:::
::: {.edu-body}
### Hon. B.Sc. in Biological Sciences & Psychology
[York University]{.edu-org}

[2013 — 2017]{.edu-years}
:::
:::

## Research & Industry Experience

::: {.edu-entry}
::: {.edu-logo}
<a href="https://www.surgicalsafety.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
  <img src="/images/sst.jpeg" alt="Surgical Safety Technologies logo" />
</a>
:::
::: {.edu-body}
### Research Assistant I
[Surgical Safety Technologies]{.edu-org}

[Toronto, ON — 2021]{.edu-years}

- Assisted on instance segmentation pipelines for surgical tool detection and tracking
- Computed inter-rater reliability (κ and ICC) for ML annotation quality across analyst teams
- Reviewed ML-for-surgical-safety literature to support internal research operations
- **Key insight:** alert hygiene and interface design determine real-world adoption of ML systems
:::
:::

::: {.edu-entry}
::: {.edu-logo}
<p class="edu-logo-icon" aria-hidden="true"><i class="fas fa-vr-cardboard"></i></p>
:::
::: {.edu-body}
### XR Interaction Research — Independent Study
[Self-directed Research]{.edu-org}

[2025–2026]{.edu-years}

- Built a fully remote web-based interaction testbed using React and TypeScript
- Conducted a within-subject hand vs. gaze pointing study with ISO 9241-9 / Fitts' law design
- Published findings as arXiv preprint (arXiv:2603.15991)
- Applied hierarchical Bayesian LBA in PyMC for verification-phase response time modeling
:::
:::

::: {.edu-entry}
::: {.edu-logo}
<p class="edu-logo-icon" aria-hidden="true"><i class="fas fa-chart-line"></i></p>
:::
::: {.edu-body}
### Surgeon Cognitive Dashboard — R&D Project
[Self-directed Research]{.edu-org}

[2025]{.edu-years}

- Designed and deployed a real-time R Shiny dashboard for surgical trainee cognitive monitoring
- Integrated XGBoost multiclass classifier with Platt scaling for cognitive state prediction
- Built three pedagogically-grounded threshold policies (Adaptive Gain, SDT, Fatigue-Adaptive)
- Live demo at: <a href="https://0z6q8c-mohammad0dastgheib.shinyapps.io/surgical-training-lab/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">shinyapps.io</a>
:::
:::
